faqs.question: Why are fly problems so common in Parkchester apartment buildings?
faqs.answer: Parkchester's large co-op complexes with shared hallways, trash chutes, and communal courtyards create multiple entry points and breeding areas for flies. The high density of residents, nearby restaurants along White Plains Road, and aging building infrastructure can contribute to persistent fly issues. Our targeted treatments address both interior infestations and exterior breeding sites specific to Parkchester's building layouts.
